anchor.snippet: a block design is an incidence structure consisting of a set together with a family of subsets known as blocks
A design is said to be balanced (up to t ) if all t -subsets of the original set occur in equally many
each element occurs in the same number of blocks (the replication number , denoted r ) and the design is said to be regular
A block design in which all the blocks have the same size (usually denoted k ) is called uniform or proper
for t = 2 they are known in the literature under the general name pairwise balanced designs (PBDs)
For t = 2 these are known as PBIBD( n ) designs , whose classes form an association scheme
Designs are usually said (or assumed) to be incomplete , meaning that the collection of blocks is not all possible k -subsets
Designs without repeated blocks are called simple
a design in which each element occurs the same total number of times is called equireplicate
The simplest type of "balanced" design ( t = 1) is known as a tactical configuration or 1-design

annotations.label: Binary matrix is incidence matrix of regular uniform design

anchor.snippet: Every binary matrix with constant row and column sums is the incidence matrix of a regular uniform block design

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: we define a 2-design (or BIBD , standing for balanced incomplete block design) B to be a family of k -element subsets of X

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: The order of a 2-design is defined to be n = r − λ

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: The complement of a 2-design is obtained by replacing each block with its complement in the point set X

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: Fisher's inequality , named after the statistician Ronald Fisher , is that b ≥ v in any 2-design

anchor.section: Pairwise balanced uniform designs (2-designs or BIBDs)
anchor.snippet: there is no choice of such a set which can make all block-sums (that is, sum of all points in a given block) constant
The unique (6,3,2)-design ( v = 6, k = 3, λ = 2) has 10 blocks
One of four nonisomorphic (8,4,3)-designs has 14 blocks with each element repeated 7 times
The unique (7,3,1)-design is symmetric and has 7 blocks with each element repeated 3 times
The case of equality in Fisher's inequality, that is, a 2-design with an equal number of points and blocks, is called a symmetric design

annotations.label: Two distinct blocks meet in λ points

anchor.snippet: in a symmetric design every two distinct blocks meet in λ points
A theorem of Ryser provides the converse
Finite projective planes are symmetric 2-designs with λ = 1 and order n > 1
For n = 2 we get a projective plane of order 2, also called the Fano plane

annotations.label: Projective planes exist for prime power orders

anchor.snippet: Projective planes are known to exist for all orders which are prime numbers or powers of primes
A biplane or biplane geometry is a symmetric 2-design with λ = 2
The order 0 biplane has 2 points (and lines of size 2
The order 1 biplane has 4 points
The order 2 biplane is the complement of the Fano plane
The order 3 biplane has 11 points (and lines of size 5
There are three biplanes of order 4
There are four biplanes of order 7
There are five biplanes of order 9
Two biplanes are known of order 11
Biplanes of orders 5, 6, 8 and 10 do not exist, as shown by the Bruck-Ryser-Chowla theorem
An Hadamard matrix of size m is an m × m matrix H whose entries are ±1
The resulting 0–1 matrix M is the incidence matrix of a symmetric
A resolvable 2-design is a BIBD whose blocks can be partitioned into sets (called parallel classes )
If a 2-( v , k ,λ) resolvable design has c parallel classes, then b ≥ v + c − 1

annotations.label: Symmetric designs have no non-trivial resolution

anchor.snippet: a symmetric design can not have a non-trivial (more than one parallel class) resolution
A solution of the famous 15 schoolgirl problem is a resolution of a 2-(15,3,1) design
a t -design B is a class of k -element subsets of X , called blocks , such that every point x in X appears in exactly r blocks

annotations.label: t-design is also s-design for s ≤ t

anchor.snippet: Any t -( v , k ,λ)-design is also an s -( v , k ,λ s )-design for any s with 1 ≤ s ≤ t
A t -( v , k ,1)-design is called a Steiner system
The derived design D p has point set X − { p } and as block set all the blocks of D which contain p with p removed
A design E is called an extension of D if E has a point p such that E p is isomorphic to D ; we call D extendable if it has an extension
If a t -( v , k , λ ) design has an extension, then k + 1 divides b ( v + 1)

annotations.label: Only extendable projective planes are orders 2 and 4

anchor.snippet: The only extendable projective planes (symmetric 2-( n 2 + n + 1, n + 1, 1) designs) are those of orders 2 and 4
Every Hadamard 2-design is extendable (to an Hadamard 3-design )
If D , a symmetric 2-( v , k ,λ) design, is extendable, then one of the following holds
A design with the parameters of the extension of an affine plane , i.e., a 3-( n 2 + 1, n + 1, 1) design, is called a finite inversive plane , or Möbius plane , of order n
An ovoid in PG(3, q ) is a set of q 2 + 1 points, no three collinear
Any inversive plane arising this way is called egglike
An example of an ovoid is the elliptic quadric , the set of zeros of the quadratic form
If q is an odd power of 2, another type of ovoid is known – the Suzuki–Tits ovoid
If q is odd, then any ovoid is projectively equivalent to the elliptic quadric in a projective geometry PG(3, q )
An n -class association scheme consists of a set X of size v together with a partition S of X × X into n + 1 binary relations
An association scheme is commutative if
A partially balanced incomplete block design with n associate classes (PBIBD( n )) is a block design based on a v -set X

annotations.label: PBIBD determines an association scheme (converse false)

anchor.snippet: A PBIBD( n ) determines an association scheme but the converse is false
Let A (3) be the following association scheme with three associate classes on the set X

annotations.label: PBIBD(1) is a BIBD; PBIBD(2) with equal λ is a BIBD

anchor.snippet: A PBIBD(1) is a BIBD and a PBIBD(2) in which λ 1 = λ 2 is a BIBD

annotations.label: Sunscreen BIBD for skin cancer trial

anchor.snippet: Suppose that skin cancer researchers want to test three different sunscreens
